task_objective: Field BRR Phase 2's second deliverable (docs/BRR_PLAN.md Phase 2, item 2: Owner-Decision Capture Flow) into live task state in the lightest viable way. When a task or cycle needs an owner decision (Class C per docs/BRR_POLICY.md, or an Owner Review Matrix row is hit), the repo should be able to record, structurally: what decision is needed, why it is needed, what options exist, and what remains blocked until the decision is made - instead of that living only as free-form prose in current_blocker or in chat. Keep this bounded to capturing and surfacing the request; do not build any automatic stop-trigger detection, owner-decision routing/notification, or acceptance-boundary enforcement - those remain separate, later Phase 2 deliverables (items 3-5).
completion_criteria: task-state.json (and schema) gain a structured way to record an owner-decision request: at minimum a needed/not-needed flag, the decision question, the reason it's needed, the options being weighed, and what stays blocked until the owner decides.
The structure is optional/nullable when no owner decision is pending, so ordinary tasks are unaffected.

The generated worker directive and advisor restart brief surface an active owner-decision request clearly when one is present, so a fresh worker or advisor session sees it without re-deriving it from prose.

The feature is demonstrated: an owner-decision request is populated for a real or realistic scenario (e.g. this BRR sub-thread's own next-step choice, or a synthetic example if no live one exists) and shown rendering correctly in both artifacts, then cleared/demonstrated absent for the normal case.

Truth-surface propagation is handled honestly across docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/BRR_PLAN.md, docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/STATE_MODEL.md, docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md, docs/REPO_GOVERNANCE.md, and README.md, updating only what this field actually makes stale.
No automatic stop-trigger detection, owner-decision routing/notification, or acceptance-boundary enforcement is introduced - this is capture and surfacing only.

Local validation remains healthy: touched generators, schema, and state files work cleanly with scripts/check-schemas.ps1, scripts/validate-cockpit-state.ps1, and scripts/doctor.ps1 on the actual returned-for-verification state.

allowed: Update schemas/task-state.schema.json and the live .cockpit/state/task-state.json to add and use one owner-decision-request structure.
Update scripts/generate-worker-directive.ps1 and scripts/generate-advisor-restart-brief.ps1 so an active owner-decision request appears clearly in generated handoff artifacts when present.
Update docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/BRR_PLAN.md, docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/STATE_MODEL.md, docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md, docs/REPO_GOVERNANCE.md, and README.md only as needed to propagate this cleanly.
Regenerate the live handoff artifacts and run the relevant local validation/health scripts to demonstrate the feature.
forbidden: Do not implement automatic stop-trigger detection (docs/BRR_PLAN.md Phase 2 item 4) - that is a separate later task.
Do not implement safe next-task drafting rules or acceptance-boundary enforcement (Phase 2 items 3 and 5) - those are separate later tasks.
Do not add any notification, routing, or automated escalation mechanism beyond making the request structurally visible in state and handoff artifacts.
Do not change the five verification verdicts, task safety classes, or BRR Phase 1 policy content unless a direct contradiction is found.
Do not rewrite archived history or retrofit old archived tasks.
Do not require the owner to manually restate BRR policy already recorded canonically.

required_evidence: Confirmation that forbidden scope was not touched.
next_action: Task 'pcc-brr2-007' is complete and verified PASS. Owner/advisor selects and drafts the next bounded task.
